title: hakea oldei
intro: Hakea oldei, also known as the black hakea, is a species of shrub native to Western Australia. It is a small tree with needle-like leaves and produces white, fragrant flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in Western Australian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. The leaves and bark of Hakea oldei can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas and tinctures, to help alleviate respiratory issues and provide relief from colds and flu.
